mnemonics.title: LCG Parameters
mnemonics.content: MICA: Multiplier (a), Increment (c), Modulus (m), Current value (X)

mnemonics.title: CSPRNG Properties
mnemonics.content: TUFF: Unpredictable, Forward secrecy, Fails next-bit test, Fast output

mnemonics.title: Random Number Quality
mnemonics.content: SPEAR: Statistical pass, Period length, Entropy source, Algorithm simplicity, Reproducibility

mnemonics.title: Box-Muller Formula
mnemonics.content: Two Uniforms go In, Two Gaussians come Out: U1,U2 → sqrt(-2lnU1)×cos(2πU2)

acronyms.usage: Software-based deterministic random number generation using algorithms like LCG, Mersenne Twister
Hardware-based generator using physical phenomena like thermal noise, radioactive decay
Simplest PRNG algorithm using linear recurrence relation X(n+1) = (aX(n) + c) mod m
PRNG suitable for security applications, unpredictable even with partial output knowledge
General term for any mechanism producing random number sequences
Most widely used PRNG with period 2^19937-1, excellent statistical properties
Describes probability distribution of continuous random variables
Probability that random variable takes value ≤ x, used in inverse transform sampling

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a grid where generated points form visible lines/planes rather than filling space uniformly - this is the lattice structure showing LCG's statistical weakness
Think of wrapping uniform points on unit circle radius 1, then stretching by sqrt(-2ln(r)) and rotating by angle - shows how circles become Gaussian bell curves
Draw a loop showing seed → state1 → state2 → ... → stateN → loop back to repeat - period shown as cycle length
Show funnel diagram where physical events (keyboard, mouse, disk) flow into entropy pool, then to CSPRNG output - illustrates non-deterministic collection

analogies.analogy: PRNG is like a deterministic playlist (same shuffle each time from same starting point), while TRNG is like actual random song selection from any genre
Seed is like setting the starting position on a board game spinner - same starting position always gives same sequence of results
Period is like a combination lock that eventually returns to starting position - longer period means more unique combinations before repeating
Like converting square building blocks into circular ones while preserving area - transforms uniform shape into Gaussian curve shape
CSPRNG is like a one-way mirror - seeing part of output tells you nothing about what's coming next; regular PRNG is like seeing through clear glass
